item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who loves body horror, was there when Starcraft ate up a lot of Filipino LAN Cafe's time, and vaguely follows MLG teams to laugh at them, I like it - to the point, and shows off some serious obsession with games.

Also, is it… supposed to be the Zerg commander avatar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want a tale series of "Para E-Sports" where two teams climb the ranks and try to get their flesh worms to look like people before the other team does, and all the dynamics and sabotage from one team to the other that would ensue. This would be a series I would read.

…Or write, maybe.

EDIT: If it wasn't obvious, I +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For those arguing that this doesn't make sense as an eSports item except in a setting with widely available anomalous tech, maybe this is a covert prototype, focused on getting the gaming down in lower-level competition and worrying about the "aesthetics" necessary for live gaming later? It certainly sounds like they're discussing newish tech in the end note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was looking for that comment. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you please expand on the E-Sports thing? I didn't get it on the first readthrough, and without that this comes across as just another body horror thingy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah but does he play a mean pinball?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noted and adjusted appropriately. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing was never a human being. It was grown in a lab. I doubt it has a sufficient level of sapience to be able to volunteer for something, let alone even really understand what's going on beyond "Beating the game makes me feel good".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't run an MRI on anything that has metallic implants. It will cause currents to run through the metal which, at the very least, will heat the metal, and if there's electronics involved it will set off (and possibly fry) the electronics. And if any of the metal is iron, the patient will be severely injured. In this situation, a CAT scan should be used instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure whether this would up the horror quotient, or tip it over into silly territory, but, given the above… have you considered implying that this meat-tube was a volunteer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Downvote rescinded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny you should say that - I got the idea for this article from having seen a number of low-quality posts that were either trolls or just plain not very well-conceived, with containment procedures along the lines of "give it an Xbox and feed it Doritos and Mountain Dew".

It got me wondering how to take a concept like an anomalous online gamer and make it SCP-worthy, and that lead me to an artificially-engineered, non-sapient meat tube that farms gold for biological stimulation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perversely1, it kind of smacks of wish fulfillment. I mean, not for the author necessarily, but for a certain kind of person who plays video games. "Wow, wouldn't it be cool if I could play seven video games at once and it would be as good as sex because girls never want to talk to me so fuck them I'll just play video games all day."

Which, conversely, a narrative thread suggesting this in the article might actually make me upvote. <.< I am a fickle puppy.

Footnotes
1. No pun intended

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: Yeah that does work, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oddly enough, that bit is actually one of the more plausible elements here- the whole "stimulating the pleasure centers" thing is actually a real thing. Really effective way of getting rats to push a button until they starve. During early medical trials of electrical brain stimulation for treating epilepsy and mental illness, there were also a couple unfortunate incidents involving misplaced electrodes demonstrating that people are only slightly more resistant to the same "Wirehead" effect.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
^ Ooooh, I didn't even think of that!

ONE NIGHT ONLY!
Five Time Reigning Champions
Br0th3rh8d.WANs_Wr4th
VS
…that rookie crawling from the vat to the leaderboards…
…The Human Centipwnr!!!
for the
2016 Augmented E-Sports Championship Title
Sponsored by MC&D
Date, time, and venue TBA.
Summon your local MC&D representative for more information or to reserve your tickets now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I find the idea of an organism designed to play video games super-good for esports inherently amusing, the kind of misuse of anomalous technology I like seeing in articles, the sexual aspect was just too silly for anything but a downvote.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: a valid point, but do we really know they are an established team in the non-anomalous world? could they be some kind of underground team who regularly competes with players from the church of the broken god and stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a short tale about this thing could be interesting. a glimpse into its mind.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: That doesn't jive with them being an established pro team.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: I think that the fact that it was created for that reason just adds to the wrongness of this thing, whoever or whatever commissioned this thing doesn't share human values.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: Yes, but this is a world where it makes sense to use this superscience to win at Counterstrike. Which means it isn't that advanced there.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: Im pretty sure that this is still beyond modern tech, I just dont think current technology could create something like this and have it actually function.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
Otherwise they'd be trying to contain the next iPhone or whatever (not to mention still have the last iPhone or whatever in containment).

Maybe that's why the real iPhones do not have that significant of updates.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation probably doesn't try to contain new technology just because it's new though. Otherwise they'd be trying to contain the next iPhone or whatever (not to mention still have the last iPhone or whatever in containment).

In the real world, this isn't even day after tomorrow tech, and it would be illegal besides. It wouldn't make much sense to try to make it in order win at e-sports for all kinds of reasons, mostly because it's wrong but also because you'd go to prison and the tournament rules wouldn't allow it anyway (and there's no incentive for the organization to change the rules).

So this is a world where it does make sense, and there's a reasonable expectation where it is a useful strategy but the Foundation still is containing it as anomaly. I'm having trouble reconciling this.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
Well, if this is just normal tech in that world then why is it in containment? The Foundation contains weird stuff, right?

The Foundation is an inherently conservative organization. It contains threats to normality, "normality" being what they have decided that the current scientific-cultural-political (fun with acronyms!) status quo is and ought to be.

A lab-bred humanoid flesh-tube with fourteen arms that gets off when it plays video games is definitely not something that fits into that status quo.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
Well, if this is just normal tech in that world then why is it in containment? The Foundation contains weird stuff, right?

I tend to assume that SCPs are written from a wainscot world that on the surface looks like ours, unless it is implied that it's from a really different world or the future or whatever. I don't think I've got a clear image of the world that this thing comes from though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Researcher: glhf

SCP-2652: u2

Researcher: I'm in Texas, where are u?

SCP-2652: The Plane Where Eyes Cannot Follow, Open, Screaming Mouths

Researcher: gg

SCP-2653: gg

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: That's all part of the metagame, though, isn't it? The team in question is already dealing with a rival in possession of something that can execute inhuman amounts of APM, and they're trying to find a way to beat it. In the long run, they're likely hoping that the people that run e-sports tournaments will legalize "players" like this organism.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
There's ways around that though- if its about money

That doesn't seem to what Smapti is saying.

+ show block
– hide block

It's definitely not gold farming. He says it's for a South Korean professional e-sports team to beat their competitors. So sooner or later they are going to have play live.

figure out some sort of hack to let it control things from the crew's "lucky vending machine" backstage and hire seven guys to hit buttons on cue so it looks like they're playing.

Presumably Blizzard or whatever has an interest already in preventing cheating like this though, even if there's just mundane tech in that soda machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes blunt is better. Just saying "it orgasms" has better immediate shock/squick factor. Dancing around it just lessens the impact by shrouding it in a bunch of curtains. Straight-out saying it forces it into the reader's face in a way they can't ignore.

"It comes when it plays video games" isn't a big enough pay-off to make hiding it worth it anyway. It's best for dawning, crushing horror (089 is a good example of this).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat.

It reminds of something Bonesaw might make in Worm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How could you "interview" such a thing? Perhaps you could feed questions to it through its sensory ports and put a keyboard in its hands, but I doubt its mind works anything like a normal human's does and it wouldn't be able to communicate in a coherent manner.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
There's ways around that though- if its about money, gold farming and multiple simultaneous low profile tournaments with several of these could rack up money fast. Worst case scenario, if you can afford to get this thing made you can afford to figure out some sort of hack to let it control things from the crew's "lucky vending machine" backstage and hire seven guys to hit buttons on cue so it looks like they're playing.

It also occurs to me that if it can play video games, it can operate a drone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for now. Interesting concept, but the execution doesn't do much for me. Maybe add some logs or interviews, let us get an idea of its behavior? Also, I feel like 'manmade' should be replaced with 'artificial' just to keep tone consistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would say that I would have upvoted so hard if not for the sudden bluntness of the orgasm thing.

If it were me I would give the description of the brain chemicals and physiological response associated with orgasm without the explicit mention of it. I think I would have found that much more satisfyingly creepy. There's a lot of interesting stuff to do with the way orgasm affects the body that would make it pretty obvious to the reader what was happening and gives extra creep factor when someone puts the pieces together themselves.

item.title: So close...
item.content:encoded: I wish there was a "When a gaming console is pluged in, the entity emits a distressed groan" or something creepy like that, instead of the orgasm thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy and rather disturbing. I didn't find the article too vague - I think most people who use the internet these days at least vaguely know what esports are and that they're a big thing in South Korea, and should be able to connect the dots.

Solid upvote.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't people notice when they are playing live? I mean I watch Starcraft tournaments sometimes and the audience can see the players.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed.

Your cybernetic gold farmer, while horrible, is missing something important.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm dangerously close to an upvote here, but its missing that last puzzle piece to push it from a freaky thing with insufficient context to an interestingly mysterious freaky thing.

Edit: figure out a way to make the above spoiler clearer and you've got my upvote.

Edit 2: that'll work. +1 for the one-entity MOBA team.

item.title: Re: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
Not sure what that communique at the bottom is, but it leaves absolutely no context.

I didn't want to be too on-the-nose about it, but the intent is as such;

+ Spoilers
- As you wish

The organism's purpose is to be really good at e-sports. A South Korean team commissioned it for the purpose of beating their competitors who were already working on/in possession of a similar organism, and it was engineered and brought into existence on their behalf by an American company - maybe Prometheus Labs, or maybe one of their competitors.

- As you wish

item.title: RE:SCP-2562
item.content:encoded:
Right, so, it's p good, just a few things I see right off the bat:

  • Grammar.
    • "…via feeding tube, and a bedpan and/or relief tube…"
      • That should be a semicolon, not a comma, mate
    • "…regularly and before and after testing…"
      • That should read "…regularly, both before and after testing…"//
    • Other such small things
  • This: "…attach video game consoles or similar devices to televisions."
    • It should not only read "…attach to…"
    • But also, those could either be HDMI, VGA, or RGB cables. Please clarify.
  • Not sure what that communique at the bottom is, but it leaves absolutely no context.

Those are the only problems I see as of right now; however, there may be more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because it has no mouth and likely must scream.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't written one of these in ages, but I had this idea a few days ago and I figured I'd throw it out there. It's probably the shortest article I've ever written, and I'm not sure whether it leaves too much unsaid, but I figured I'd throw it out there and see how it goes.
